question_text: In the lens setup shown below, an object is placed in front of a convex lens at a distance equal to twice the focal length (2F). The image formed by such a setup is:

answer_key.explanation: For an object at 2F before a convex lens, the image is real, inverted, and of the same size at 2F on the other side — students must recall the rule; the diagram only fixes the setup.
answer_key.related_concept: Image formation by a convex lens

visual.caption: Object before a convex lens.
visual.altText: Principal axis with a single upright object at distance twenty centimetres on the left of a convex lens of focal length ten centimetres.
